titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sheldon Family Papers.
titlestmt.author: Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 21, 2026 at 23:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection contains correspondence, military papers, legal papers, printed material, photographs, journals, and other papers relating to members of the Cooper, Tweed, Savage, and Sheldon families.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 8, Folder 2 (correspondence): Restricted fragile. Item awaiting treatment.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1739-1917. II. Family papers, 1683-1945.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sheldon Family papers. General collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Sheldon family papers and photographs (Coll. 4282) at Maine Historical Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Sheldon Family papers document the history of Anne Dudley Sheldon Swift's (1916-2003) family. The papers come from both her father and mother's sides of the family, the Sheldons and the Coopers respectfully. Anne's family papers capture snapshots of life in Maine, Massachusetts, and Connecticut over several generations including significant moments in American history such as the Revolutionary and Civil wars.

Harrison Tweed Sheldon (1883-1933)--Anne's father--was born in New Haven, CT. He graduated from Yale University in 1905 and Yale Law School in 1908. Harrison married Alice Cooper Stanton (1879-1967) in 1915. The couple, and later their family, frequently took trips to the Katahdin region of Maine to camp, hike, canoe, and fish. Both Harrison and Anne are buried in the historic Grove Street Cemetery in New Haven, CT.

The Cooper branch of Anne's family includes the Savage and Tudor families. Papers relating to the Cooper family include correspondence between James S. Cooper and his father John Cooper; William Savage's journals documenting sea travel to the southern hemisphere; and official notes from the Committee of Correspondence for Boston, written by town clerk Reverend William Cooper.

The Sheldon branch of Anne's family includes the Tweed, Pond, and Jones families. Papers relating to the Sheldon family include correspondence between Theodore H. Sheldon and his family members including his wife Emily, and his brother Winthrop; military commission documents; and financial records for the family cattle business.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sheldon Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into two sub-series: Cooper family correspondence; and Sheldon family correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence created by members of Alice Cooper Stanton Sheldon's family. Correspondents include family members, friends, and business associates.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 8, Folder 2: Restricted fragile. Item awaiting treatment.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged into four groupings: James Sullivan Cooper correspondence; John Cooper correspondence; William Savage correspondence; and General Cooper family correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence from the Cooper branch of the family, which includes the Savage and Tudor families.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 8, Folder 2: Restricted fragile. Item awaiting treatment.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is organized into outgoing and incoming correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is organized into outgoing and incoming correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this grouping are arranged chronologically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 8, Folder 2: Restricted fragile. Item awaiting treatment.

did.unittitle: Letter from Thomas Cooper to William Cooper

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Item awaiting treatment.

did.unittitle: Letter from Reverend William Cooper to William Cooper Jr.
Letter from Mary Cooper to Caroline Cooper
Letter from S.D.F. to Thomas Savage
Letter from James Ingersoll Wyer Jr. to his sister
Letter from Charles W. Cooper to Emily [Tweed?]
Letter from Henry W. Longfellow to Henry W. [Thomett?]
Copy of a letter from Thomas Cooper to William Cooper

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This sub-series is arranged into four groupings: Catherine Winthrop Jones Pond correspondence; Emily Tweed Sheldon correspondence; Theodore Hulburd Sheldon correspondence; and General Sheldon family correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence from the Sheldon branch of the family which includes the Tweed, Pond, and Jones families.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is organized into outgoing and incoming correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Outgoing correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is organized into outgoing and incoming correspondence.

arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is organized into outgoing and incoming correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Outgoing correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this grouping are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Letter from Joshua Tweed to Betty Pratt
Letter from H.P. Laper to Ann Dudley Sheldon
Letter from [Huldah Ann Pond?] to [Charles Harrison Tweed?]
Letter from Ann Dudley Jones Sheldon
Letter from G.H. Foster to Huldah Ann Pond
Letter to Alice Cooper Stanton Sheldon

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into four sub-series: Cooper family papers; Savage family papers; Sheldon family papers; and Unsorted family papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal and professional papers related to the lives of the Cooper, Savage, Sheldon, and Tweed family members.
Materials include military commissions, journals, legal papers, financial records, and photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Broadside of a letter to Reverend William Cooper
Notes from the Committee of Correspondence for Boston written by William Cooper
William Albies[?] deposition relating to John Cooper
Copy of a petition to the General Court of Massachusetts by John Cooper
Address from the Committee of Plantation to John Cooper
"A Topographical Description of Machias" by John Cooper
Mary E. Cooper Savage recipe book
Monody on the Death of Samuel Cooper
"On the Patronage of Genius" by James Savage

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Memoranda on the marriage of Theodore Jones and Katharine Winthrop Sargent
[Winthrop D. Sheldon?] nine month campaign journal
Harrison Tweed financial statements and expenses
Theodore H. Sheldon cattle raising contract and expenses
"the Death and Resurrection of Lazarus" by O.P. Tweed

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Mail stage time tables from Boston to Albany and Philadelphia to Jersey City
Florence Nightingale Society of Calais benefit broadside
